rationale: The citing paper devotes substantial text to Kruger (2007), explicitly organizing part of the rejoinder around his three proposed reasons for persistence in geographic share differences. It uses Kruger's ideas to shape the paper's conceptual discussion of mechanisms (marketing allocation and share-distribution feedback) and future research priorities. It also directly evaluates and qualifies Kruger's retailer-confound conjecture with additional analysis across 30 categories, reporting limited support overall. This is therefore more than a passing mention and functions as a meaningful qualification of the cited comment.
relation_description: The citing paper engages Kruger (2007) as a key mechanism-oriented comment and incorporates parts of his reasoning into its interpretation of persistence in geographic variation. It then narrows that contribution by testing the retailer interaction claim more broadly and concluding that retailer effects explain only a limited share of variation.
